pattern: Filling the bags that need the fewest rocks first is a greedy that provably maximises how many reach full capacity within a fixed budget.
hints: Each bag needs capacity minus current rocks more to be full. Some need almost nothing, some need a lot.
Spending your budget on the cheapest deficits first lets you complete the most bags.
Compute every deficit, sort ascending, and fill them in order while the remaining budget covers the next one; count how many you complete.

edge_inputs.description: budget completes some but not all bags
single bag already full needs nothing
single bag needs exactly the whole budget
budget covers all small deficits but not the big one
tiny budget fills only the cheapest deficit

stress.description: large set of bags with random deficits and a moderate budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    capacity = []
    rocks = []
    for _ in range(n):
        cap = rng.randint(1, 1000)
        capacity.append(cap)
        rocks.append(rng.randint(0, cap))
    additional = rng.randint(1, n * 50)
    return [capacity, rocks, additional]
stress.note: rocks always within capacity per the constraints; budget large enough to fill several
